mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during documented rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that compress corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power when the baseline unemployment rate falls below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month declines in real retail sales and housing starts indicate a synchronized contraction in consumer and housing demand, which reduces near-term corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint decline fails to predict negative returns when GDP growth remains above 2% annualized, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant with a p-value exceeding 0.10 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary easing cycle reflects improving factory utilization and output growth, which triggers short-term momentum buying and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening cycles, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical edge with a t-statistic below 1.5 when industrial production growth exceeds 1% monthly.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index rises by more than 0.4% month-over-month, inflation expectations accelerate and force markets to price in tighter future monetary policy, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55% of high-inflation months, or the predictive relationship vanishes when core inflation measures are substituted for headline CPI.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm broad-based labor market strength, which supports consumer spending and corporate profitability, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails when payroll growth is concentrated in low-wage sectors, or the joint condition yields a negative return frequency exceeding 50% in walk-forward validation periods.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate demand and corporate earnings momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter as earnings revisions propagate through equity valuations.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 5-year windows, or GDP growth fails to predict equity returns when financial stress indicators are elevated above historical medians.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sharp contraction in manufacturing output that precedes earnings downgrades and triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The threshold fails to predict negative returns when the decline is driven by seasonal adjustments, or positive returns occur in greater than 65% of triggered cases during out-of-sample testing.
